--- SOURCE 1: STEAM Hub ---
URL: https://theeducationpartnership.org/steam-hub/

SUMMARY:
The Education Partnership's STEAM Hub equips our partner teachers with classroom kits of ready-to-use, hands-on resources for teaching engaging lessons.

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://theeducationpartnership.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/05/logo.png)

# STEAM Hub

### We provide school supplies for a high-tech future

In today’s world, students need more than just pencils and paper to succeed in school and beyond. The Education Partnership’s **STEAM Hub** equips our partner teachers with classroom kits of ready-to-use, hands-on resources for teaching engaging lessons in Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Math.

## 

### STEAM Lending Library

Lendable preK through 12th grade STEAM kits for exploring robotics, electronics, biology, art, chemistry, physics and more.

### STEAM Possible Packs

Creative STEAM materials packaged as ready-to-use kits for engaging students in hands-on learning activities.

### Matt's Maker Space

Check out our Matt’s Maker Space on-site at TEP. Use our tools and resources and attend partner-provided professional development sessions.

## We’re going beyond the basics

Learn how STEAM school supplies can make a real difference in our community.

--- SOURCE 2: Community Partnerships and STEM Education ---
URL: https://21stcented.com/the-importance-of-community-partnerships-in-advancing-stem-education/

SUMMARY:
To advance STEM education, schools can establish community partnerships with local businesses, research institutions, and non-profit organizations, combining the efforts of these institutions to give students hands-on experiences that allow them to put their skills to the test in a safe and controlled environment. Let’s explore the importance of community partnerships in advancing STEM education and how they can boost student engagement, ensure national science progress, and provide diverse opportunities for students to encounter the world of science and technology. Community partnerships also help ensure each student has a chance to be part of STEM education. Community partnerships are crucial for providing students with effective STEM education and resources. Community partnerships are essential in advancing STEM education by providing students with real-world experiences.

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://px.ads.linkedin.com/collect/?pid=6953636&fmt=gif)
![](https://21stcented.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/21stCenturyEd-logo-transparent-bg.png)

# The Importance of Community Partnerships in Advancing STEM Education

![community partnerships](https://21stcented.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/pexels-katerina-holmes-5905918.jpg)

STEM education prepares students to succeed in the rapidly evolving world of technology and innovation. Building communication, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills prepares students to contribute to their jobs and communities. To advance STEM education, schools can establish community partnerships with local businesses, research institutions, and non-profit organizations, combining the efforts of these institutions to give students hands-on experiences that allow them to put their skills to the test in a safe and controlled environment. Let’s explore the importance of community partnerships in advancing STEM education and how they can boost student engagement, ensure national science progress, and provide diverse opportunities for students to encounter the world of science and technology.

## What Are Community Partnerships?

A community partnership is when businesses and organizations pair up with schools to [improve STEM education](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5512595/). These partnerships give students learning opportunities by sharing resources and knowledge. For example, a local tech company might donate computers to a school or offer workshops for students to learn to code. These collaborations make STEM education more practical and exciting, which helps students gain the skills they need for their future.

## Benefits of Community Partnerships in STEM Education

Building community partnerships is an essential part of effective STEM education. These partnerships offer schools and students various opportunities and advantages.

### Greater Access to Diverse Resources

One of the main [benefits of community partnerships in STEM education](https://www.monash.edu/__data/assets/pdf_file/0006/2093352/Community-partnerships-for-fostering-student-interest-and-engagement-in-STEM.pdf) is that they improve access to educational resources and experiences. When schools work with places like local businesses and non-profit organizations, they can offer students more comprehensive STEM education, resources, and experience.

Partnerships like this give students access to the latest advancements in technology and research, effectively preparing them to operate in a technologically advanced workplace. By providing students access to recent research, the latest technology, and industry experts, students can better understand STEM principles, advancing STEM education.

### Offer New Clubs, Activities, and Extracurricular Experiences

Community partnerships also offer additional financial support to schools, allowing them to invest in STEM programs. This extra investment will enable schools to create more comprehensive STEM programs, including STEM-focused clubs, workshops, seminars, and competitions. These in-school activities and programs offer students a safe environment to test their skills and knowledge, allowing them to grow and develop their interests and mastery of STEM principles.

### Improve Inclusivity of STEM Education

One of the main problems with STEM education is the need for more diversity and inclusivity. Community partnerships also help ensure each student has a chance to be part of STEM education. These collaborations can offer virtual learning, scholarships, and mentorship programs, giving underserved students access to advanced STEM education. These partnerships can also provide role models for students who might not see people like themselves in STEM careers. By working with various partners, schools can teach STEM subjects in ways that are more interesting and relatable to a wide range of students.

### Provide Real-World Experiences to Students

Community partnerships also provide students with real-world experiences, allowing them to learn about potential careers and explore their occupational interests. Internships and job-shadowing opportunities in the community show students what working in different STEM fields is like. Students can also work on projects that deal with real-world problems or collaborate with professionals on research. This helps them develop essential problem-solving and critical-thinking skills for their future careers.

Community partnerships are crucial for providing students with effective STEM education and resources. They foster the development of a highly skilled workforce by providing increased opportunities for students to test their skills and interests.

## Examples of Community Partnerships

Schools can create community partnerships with various organizations and institutions to further STEM education. Here are a few examples of what these partnerships might look like.

### Local Business Collaborations

Businesses in your community can play a vital role in supporting STEM education. Local companies sponsor STEM events, clubs, and competitions, allowing them to invest in the next generation of leaders. These businesses can also contribute to curriculum development by offering industry-focused workshops to students, introducing their companies, and describing what they do. They can also provide guest speaker events, allowing students to gain industry knowledge and insights. These collaborations can help students learn relevant skills and knowledge for their future careers.

### Partnerships with Research Institutions

Collaborating with local research institutions allows students to participate in relevant research projects, broadening their understanding of the scientific process and developing critical thinking skills. These partnerships can also open doors to collaborative grants and funding opportunities and provide access to advanced resources and expertise.

### Non-profit Organization Involvement

[Non-profit organizations can significantly support STEM education](https://www.forbes.com/sites/forbestechcouncil/2019/07/23/five-nonprofits-driving-a-revolution-in-stem-education/?sh=355334f84c44), often making it more exciting and meaningful for students. For example, a non-profit might create special programs or camps where students can learn about coding, robotics, or environmental science in a fun, hands-on way. These programs can make STEM subjects more engaging and help students discover new interests.

Another way non-profit organizations can get involved in STEM education is by offering teacher training and resources. They can organize workshops, conferences, or online courses where teachers can learn the latest teaching methods and strategies for STEM subjects. This support helps teachers stay updated and provide the best possible education for their students.

Some non-profit organizations focus on making STEM education more accessible to everyone, regardless of their background. They might offer scholarships or financial aid to students who want to attend STEM camps or other learning opportunities but can’t afford them. They can also work to increase awareness of STEM careers among underrepresented groups by organizing special events or mentorship programs.

Community partnerships are essential in advancing STEM education by providing students with real-world experiences. By fostering diversity and inclusion, these collaborations can help prepare the next generation of scientists, engineers, and innovators for the challenges of the 21st century. Communities must establish and maintain strong partnerships to ensure that students receive the best possible STEM education and have the opportunity to encounter the fascinating world of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. Working together can build a brighter future for our students and the nation.

## Looking for Accessible STEM Education?

As we move into the twenty-first century, STEM subjects are becoming essential for students to learn. Getting familiar with technology and learning to use and create it opens a wide field of jobs. Here at [21stCentEd](https://21stcented.com/), we are passionate about helping young people prepare for a bright future in which their STEM skills will help them find jobs that will be relevant in this digital age.

##### About 21st Century Ed

At 21st Century Ed, our mission is to provide all students access to a comprehensive STEM education early, often, and everywhere.

![](https://21stcented.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/Book.webp)
![Latino students](https://21stcented.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/STEM-CENTURY-NABSE.jpeg)

--- SOURCE 3: Partnerships - Steam Dream ---
URL: https://www.steamdreamlabs.org/partnerships

SUMMARY:
Steam Dream seeks partners for K-12, higher education, and corporate partnerships, including financial, in-kind, and strategic advising.

FULL CONTENT:
![Steam Dream](//images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/1b2d2d3b-ab70-4ffa-b01b-be4f86ea1c75/92dc0d_eaf368ef2d594b3597a84b772fcf24f5_mv2-_1_-removebg-preview.png?format=1500w)
![Steam Dream](//images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/1b2d2d3b-ab70-4ffa-b01b-be4f86ea1c75/92dc0d_eaf368ef2d594b3597a84b772fcf24f5_mv2-_1_-removebg-preview.png?format=1500w)

# **Partnership**

STEAM & Dream Liberation Labs is dedicated to closing the STEM exposure gap and cultivating a diverse future in science, technology, engineering, arts, and math. We're looking for passionate partners who share our vision to empower Black and Brown youth through culturally relevant STEM education.

### **Here's how you can join our mission**

### **Begin a K-12 Partnership**

Integrate our project-based learning programs into your curriculum, either in-school or after-school. STEAM & Dream can provide engaging STEAM programming for your students. We can bring any of our 5 tracks to your middle school(high school in the future).

### **Current Partnering Schools**

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/3a2d4f02-c842-4ec4-a38b-0cdbb5fa2227/UCHicago.jpeg)
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/e3bc75f9-38d9-4efb-b440-c9228cfad6b2/RonaldMcNair.jpeg)
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/1ddbc5a8-9b15-4c3c-bf68-f5ca8037c476/Perspectives.png)
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/7ee8529e-17da-49d7-8f19-0ae09b74acbd/GaryComer.jpeg)

### **Begin a Higher Education Partnership**

Partner with us by connecting your STEM, education, and youth development students with opportunities to mentor and inspire future generations as instructors. This can count as an internship for students. Early exposure to STEM while lead to a pipeline of future college students who are informed and interested in STEM pathways.

### **Current University & College Partners**

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/b3657b27-113b-498b-a63b-3975bd563d3e/UIC.png)
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/aaa4637d-0d32-45ad-bb56-7dce599a90e3/ROosevelt+Unviersity.png)
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/16be7fdb-79e5-4feb-83a9-412d93a773fa/ChicagoState.png)
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/6423b773-102d-4b1c-aac9-7e7bcf069476/RUsh.png)
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/d2bc12fc-595e-4534-848c-023e92b09135/DePaul.png)
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/66a8b3008cb2d5516f86d3f7/4c1613fe-90d9-4a4c-9a54-b7868d015331/National+Louis.png)

### **Begin a Corporate Partnership**

**Financial Sponsorships and Donations**

Provide monetary contributions to support the development and execution of our STEM labs.

Fund scholarships for students from underrepresented communities to participate in our programs.

Sponsor specific events, such as our culminating Future Ceremony.

**In-Kind Donations**

Donate technology and equipment such as computers, tablets, software, lab materials, and other STEM-related resources.

Provide office supplies, furniture, and other operational necessities.

**Event Hosting and Sponsorship**

Host fundraising events, workshops, or seminars at corporate facilities.

Sponsor community events, such as STEM fairs, hackathons, or science festivals that align with our mission.

**Strategic Advising**

Provide pro-bono consulting services in areas such as strategic planning, fundraising strategy, marketing, and operational efficiency.

Join our advisory board or committees to offer expertise and guidance.

**Community Engagement**

Collaborate with us on community outreach programs to raise awareness and interest in STEM among Black and Brown youth.

Support initiatives that promote diversity, equity, and inclusion within the STEM fields.

***Together, we can build a future where Black and Brown youth see themselves thriving in STEM fields and become the innovators and leaders of tomorrow. Contact us today to discuss a partnership!***

**STEAM & Dream Liberation Labs, 501(c)3**

**Social Links**

**Contact**

**Email: Info**[**@steamdreamlabs.org**](mailto:S.tillmanFranklin@steamdreamlabs.org)

© 2024 The Steam & Dream Liberation Labs. All Rights Reserved.

STEAM Partners

#### Share on: [facebook](#) [X/Twitter](#) [LinkedIn](#) [Pinterest](#)

Check out our exciting partners Exploring by the Seat of Your Pants, the Tech Bandits, and the STEAM Sisters!

![Exploring by the Seat of Your Pants logo](/sites/default/files/2020-05/EBTSOYP%20Logo.jpg.png "EBTSOYP logo")

## Exploring by the Seat of Your Pants

[Exploring By The Seat of Your Pants](http://www.exploringbytheseat.com/ "Exploring by the Seat of Your Pants") is beaming adventure, science and conservation into classrooms and homes around the world! Join us daily for free, interactive programs featuring scientists, explorers and amazing places all around the globe, live on screen on our [Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/c/Exploringbytheseatofyourpants/live "YouTube Live - Exploring by the Seat of Your Pants")! Learn more on our website and check out a full slate of our past programs at <https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCvQkEzcH9f_9OylEdmALiWg>

![The Tech Bandits logo](/sites/default/files/2020-05/techbandits_shirt-techterrors1g.png "Tech Bandits logo")

## The Tech Bandits

Curiosity is contagious. The Tech Bandits are all about inspiring excitement and passion for Science Technology, Engineering Arts and Math. Our free club sessions and online content is a magnificent, mad, mess of curiosity driven exploration that follows all sorts of random inspiring STEAM connections...often with a fascinating focus on Assistive Tech. Our Banditry covers all sorts of robotics, biotech, chemistry, programming, big data, AI, computer security, gaming, design and so much more... plus when STEAM is linked to adaptive and rehabilitative systems, for people with disabilities or the elderly; our Bandits get to see the direct connection between the technologies they're playing with and improving their fellow human's lives.

***Contagious Curiosity - Inspired, Lifelong Learning***

<https://thetechbandits.com/>

![The Steam Sisters Logo](/sites/default/files/2020-05/Steam%20Sisters%20Logo.png "The Steam Sisters Logo")

## The STEAM Sisters

We’re the STEAM Sisters! We love sharing our passion for STEAM [Science, Technology, Engineering, Art, and Math] and bringing you along on our adventures! We believe STEAM is for everyone, and it is our mission to encourage youth to discover the magic and excitement for themselves, in engaging and accessible ways. We aim to inspire kids to shoot for the stars as they realize STEAM careers can be anything they want them to be. Join us as we go full STEAM ahead – through hands-on activities, digital and broadcast media, and at an event near you!

Check out our weekly STEAMStories on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLSP5K0wIViUdxtRNyENGZZR6sgN9nEBw9 "STEAMStories - YouTube").

Watch the Boredom Busters that we worked with Shaftesbury Kids on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLSP5K0wIViUdnGDyfQ6ZYI87k3FZqaTSH "Boredom Busters - YouTube").

[Instagram - the STEAM Sisters](https://www.instagram.com/steam.sisters/ "The STEAM Sisters - Instagram")

Sandhya Mylabathula and Swapna Mylabathula (IG: @steam.sisters) are the STEAM Sisters! When they’re not on exciting STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Art, and Math) adventures, they research concussions as PhD and MD/PhD students respectively at the University of Toronto (but let’s face it, that’s still a STEAM adventure!). Swapna is a McLaughlin Scholar, and her research includes work on concussion policy and concussion in hockey. Sandhya is a returning CIHR Fellow in Public Health Policy, and has investigated concussion risk factors, policy, education, sex/gender, and management. Sandhya and Swapna co-developed a national concussion strategy and consulted on provincial concussion policy, and co-delivered a TEDx Talk on the topic. They are honoured to have been recognized as two of the top 50 Most Influential Torontonians (GridTO Magazine) and as Urban Heroes (Toronto Community News) for their contributions. They love working with youth as Outreach Coordinators for Let’s Talk Science, and are keen about sharing their passion for all things STEAM through their work in children's television (Science Max, Finding Stuff Out, STEAM Stars, Boredom Busters), and recently launched their own initiative, active on social media as @steam.sisters on Instagram and @steam\_sisters on Twitter, as well as in person at events, talks, and conferences. Sandhya has also created and contributed to interactive STEAM exhibits, including at the University of Toronto and the Ontario Science Centre. Swapna and Sandhya are also founding members of Medicine in Motion Podcast, and Swapna has been a show host and content creator for Raw Talk Podcast for the last three seasons. Sport (particularly hockey!) is one of their many hobbies, and is what originally sparked their interest in concussions. They also enjoy playing the violin and sitar, learning new languages, as well as learning to cook new dishes from different places! :)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Building partnerships with local businesses, non-profits, and educational institutions enhances STEAM learning, providing students with practical experiences and access to industry resources and expertise.

Organizations like The Education Partnership’s STEAM Hub offer affordable, ready-to-use classroom kits, ensuring teachers have necessary materials for engaging projects in science, technology, engineering, arts, and math.

Community partnerships provide professional development workshops for educators, equipping them with innovative teaching strategies and resources to effectively deliver STEAM education to diverse student populations.
